rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MEAD VS. SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Judmgnt On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 24, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Judgment on the pleadings is not the proper procedural mechanism for disposing of this cause of action. However, pursuant to CCP sec. 436, the Court strikes the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the First Amended Complaint because it is not "filed in conformity with¿an order of the court." On March 20, 2011, this Court granted Defendant's Anti-SLAPP as to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action. (See Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. A.) The granting of an Anti-SLAPP is an "automatic dismissal" without leave to amend. (Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275, 1294; see also Simmons v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 1968, 1073-74.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FATIMA GUILLEN VS. UNIVISION TELEVISION GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respones To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FATIMA GUILLEN's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as to Request for Production No. 2. The statement is sufficiently definite to understand the category of documents requested. The protective order does not provide for redaction of contact information or contain an "opt out" clause for contact information on the wage statements. The privacy interest of the employees is sufficiently protected by a designation of the documents as "confidential." The procedure used in Belaire-West would be an alternative to the protective order. The terms of the notice to the employees are not before the court but would be critical to afford a balance of interests. Insofar as the Belaire-West procedure and notice were not incorporated in the protective order, it is not applicable here. Sanctions granted in the amount of $500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FATIMA GUILLEN VS. UNIVISION TELEVISION GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft To Respond To Special Interrogatories, Set 1;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF FATIMA GUILLEN's Motion To Compel Defendant To Respond To Special Interrogatories, Set 1; Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted insofar as redacted documents have been produced by responding party. The objections as to vague, ambiguous, and overbroad are overruled and not well taken. The redactions potentially preclude application of C.C.P. Section 2030.230. However, provided that responding party provides complete unredacted documents in response to Plaintiff's Request for Production No. 1 and certifies the documents are complete, then a designation pursuant to C.C.P. §2030.230 would be a sufficient to the interrogatories. Since the document request only seeks a single category of documents, defined as to time and employee classification (non-exempt employees), no further designation of the documents would be required in the course of responding to the interrogatory. Request for sanctions denied. The Court finds the response under C.C.P. Section 2030.230 was substantially justified. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRAD D SANDERS VS. EDWARD ZUSMAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ATTORNEY LICENSED TO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For An Order Compelling Deft To Comply With Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF BRAD SANDERS' Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant To Comply With Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. This is a motion to compel ESI information that has already been provided in printed form. Accordingly, the parties are to specifically address the factors listed in C.C.P. §2031.310(d) through (g), how and where the ESI information is stored, and the necessity of ESI production of possibly duplicative information. Note: the Court does not consider the issue of waiver of objections to be germane.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOYCE VALENTINE VS. HEALD COLLEGE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 24, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANTS HEALD COLLEGE LLC'S and CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Case The motion is granted. Whether the Court relies on the December 15, 2008 or January 13, 2009 arbitration agreement, they both incorporate by reference JAMS Rule 11(c). JAMS Rule 11(c) provides that: "Jurisdictional and arbitrability disputes, including disputes over the formation, existence, validity, interpretation or scope of the agreement under which Arbitration is sought, and who are proper Parties to the Arbitration, shall be submitted to and ruled on by the Arbitrator. Unless the relevant law requires otherwise, the Arbitrator has the authority to determine jurisdiction and arbitrability issue as a preliminary matter." (Cute Declaration, para. 7, Ex. C.) Accordingly, the parties agreed to arbitrate arbitrability and the arbitrator will decide as a preliminary matter whether defendant waived the right to arbitrate and whether the promotion letter of September 9, 2009, supersedes the parties' agreement to arbitrate. See Oracle Am., Inc. v. Myriad Grp. A.G., 724 F.3d 1069, 1074 (9th Cir. 2013); Rodriguez v. Am. Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1123; Dream Theater, Inc. v. Dream Theater (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 547, 557. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANTS HAMRICK & EVANS, LLP, A. HAMRICK, KENNETH GREENE, LAW OFFICES OF KENNETH C. GREENE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Stipulation and Order to File Second Amended Complaint will be signed by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LUISA GUTIERREZ VS. STRATEGIC HEALTHCARE SYSTEMS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, line 11. PLAINTIFF LUISA GUTIERREZ'S MOTION For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint. Granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff may file its proposed Second Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC. VS. PV FOOD LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, April 24, 2014, line 12. PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Off calendar. BiRite Restaurant Supply Inc. did not timely file proof of service for the motion. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) =(302/MJM)
